sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Clicking on the N U-shape at (21, 39) didn't change the N block. It's still N=5. This is strange because earlier, clicking on the N U-shape toggled the N block between 5 and 14.
Wait, maybe the U-shapes only toggle the source blocks when clicked on specific pixels within the U-shape. Let me check which pixel I clicked on.
The N U-shape is at rows 20-22, cols 38-40. I clicked on (21, 39), which is the center pixel of the U-shape. But the center pixel of the U-shape is not part of the U-shape itself (it's the missing pixel in the square ring).
Let me check the ascii for the N U-shape:
row 20: NNN
row 21: N N
row 22: NNN
The center pixel at (21, 39) is the missing pixel in the square ring. So clicking on the center pixel doesn't toggle the source block. I need to click on one of the N pixels in the U-shape.
Let me try clicking on (20, 39), which is the top-center pixel of the N U-shape.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# I clicked on the center pixel of the U-shape, which is the missing pixel
# Let me click on an actual N pixel in the U-shape instead
# N U-shape: row 20: NNN, row 21: N N, row 22: NNN
# Let me click on (20, 39) which is the top-center N pixel

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 39}])
print("After clicking N U-shape at (20, 39):")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] != 7:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  N id={n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
After clicking N U-shape at (20, 39):
  N id=2: pixels=5, rows=9-11, cols=28-29
